acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:277-282 shows Gabriel's line landing through a concrete bodily, sensory, or spatial consequence on Marisol, and does not summarize the estrangement in abstract retrospective terms. Fail if the beat still explains what the line 'carried' or summarizes years of history instead of staging the impact.
problem: The rebind confrontation peaks on Gabriel's cutting line, then the narration partly steps out of scene to explain what the moment 'carried' in abstract multi-year terms. That softens the chapter's hardest emotional beat instead of letting the wound stay bodily and immediate, which is below the style bible's physical-consequence-first standard.
rewrite_direction: Revise the aftermath of Gabriel's line in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:277 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:279-282. Keep the focus inside Marisol's body, the aisle, and the pressure of the closing car; replace the explanatory summary about years of distance with one or two concrete reactions or memory shards that let the estrangement register without authorial unpacking.

acceptance_test: Pass if, after revision, no more than two of the cited passages still rely on the same negation-and-qualification template, and each of those moments reads with a distinct sentence shape on the page. Fail if three or more of the cited passages still use the same 'not X, not Y' / 'There was no X. There was not even Y' construction.
problem: A repeated negation-and-qualification sentence template recurs often enough to read as autopilot: 'not X, not Y, only Z' and adjacent variants. The recurrence flattens otherwise sharp prose and trips the constitution's repetition threshold for sentence construction.
rewrite_direction: Vary the syntax at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:83, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:199, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:279. Keep the meanings, but replace most of the repeated negated double-qualification beats with more direct concrete description or different sentence shapes so each impact point earns its own rhythm.

acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can move from the chapter-01 boundary excerpt into the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:35, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:297 without inventing an unseen reset in Gabriel's custody state, and if the accusation uses a same-shift time marker unless a day break is explicitly staged on the page. Fail if Gabriel is still described as awaiting final car assignment after the prior boundary has already placed him in Car 8, or if "yesterday" remains without a clearly established elapsed day.
problem: The chapter seam breaks continuity. This scene reintroduces Gabriel as if he is still waiting for final car assignment and then has him say Marisol recognized him in intake "yesterday," but the boundary material for the prior chapter ends with him already inside Car 8 at final two-minute and this chapter still closes during the same southbound staging window. The result reads like an unmarked rewind rather than a continuation.
rewrite_direction: Rebuild the custody setup around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:35, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:87, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:297 so Gabriel's location and timing follow directly from the prior-chapter tail. Either pull him out of an already-loaded car for medical, or explicitly establish an unload/requeue before the lane-three description; then replace the time marker at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:297 with one that matches the actual elapsed time.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:17, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:57, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:339 all describe an anatomically compatible side-and-organ pairing, and Celia's scan result answers the same risk Marisol used to justify the transfer. Fail if the chapter still pairs right-side rib trauma with a splenic threat.
problem: The medical logic puts a splenic injury on Gabriel's right side. Marisol identifies right-rib trauma, palpates the right side, and then twice invokes splenic danger, including in Celia's scan read. Because the spleen is left-sided, the pretext currently reads as anatomically wrong rather than plausibly improvised under pressure.
rewrite_direction: Align the injury side and organ logic at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:17, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:53, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:57, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:339. If the bruise stays on the right side, change the concern and scan language to a right-sided risk such as liver trauma, kidney trauma, or generic internal bleeding. If splenic danger is the intended lever, move the bruise, palpation, and later scan references to the left side everywhere.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised paragraphs anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:95, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:205 no longer produce three or more near-identical negation-then-reframe beats when read in sequence. Fail if a reader still encounters that same corrective sentence shape in three or more of those four spots.
problem: A negation-then-correction sentence pattern turns into a visible tic: "It was not refusal. It was arithmetic," "It was not because the recorder looked expensive," "He did not lean on her. He would have fallen first," and "He was not accusing for effect. He was keeping accounts." The chapter uses this architecture often enough that the prose starts announcing its own emphasis instead of letting image and rhythm do the work.
rewrite_direction: Recast the repeated beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:95, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:205 so at least two of them land through fresh syntax, image, or direct assertion instead of the same explicit negation-and-reframe move. Keep the distinctions, but vary the sentence architecture.

acceptance_test: Pass if, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:111 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:209, at least one exchange after Paloma's first challenge materially changes another character's practical suspicion or imposes an immediate new action on Marisol before she leaves the table. Fail if the scene still ends with Paloma and Ivo in the same operative position they occupied when Paloma first asked what she was carrying.
evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:111 (Paloma asks what Marisol is carrying); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:117 (Paloma pushes again); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:185 (Ivo asks whether it is just paperwork); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:197 (he exits after offering cover); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:209 (Marisol leaves the cafeteria with no sharper cost attached to either coworker).
problem: Once Paloma and Ivo register that Marisol is wrong-footed, the meal scene keeps producing excellent worker texture but too little leverage change. Marisol lies, both friends notice, and the scene still releases them without a new concrete suspicion, consequence, or forced choice. At this review standard, that reads as atmospheric drift inside a chapter whose engine is supposed to be appearing normal among friends while the possibility of quiet correction dies.
rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:185, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:197 so the concealment escalates instead of plateauing. Keep the off-duty meal and the worker-specific humor, but convert part of the banter stretch around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:137 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:147 into pressure that changes what Paloma or Ivo now suspects, or forces Marisol to leave early / act differently because the lie is no longer holding cleanly.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised versions of the cited paragraphs reduce the repeated `because X and because Y` / `not X ... not Y ... just Z` pattern to no more than one instance across those targeted beats, while each paragraph still makes Marisol's motive legible on first read. Fail if multiple key beats still rely on the same qualifier-heavy sentence shape.
evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:3 (double-because motive sentence); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:5 (self-correcting negation: habit, not paranoia, not only habit); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:69 (double-because motive sentence); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:135 (double-because motive sentence); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:211 (double-because motive sentence); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:255 (not defiance / not sympathy); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:283 (not hope exactly); snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:285 (nothing dramatic / just the little household matter).
problem: The chapter reuses the same explanatory qualification machinery at too many major beats: doubled causal clauses ("because X and because Y") and self-correcting negation ("not X ... not Y ... just Z"). Each instance works locally, but together they make Marisol's close-third intelligence feel patterned rather than freshly pressured, flattening rhythm exactly where the chapter wants surprise and force.
rewrite_direction: Revise the sentence architecture in place at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:3, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:5, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:211, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:255, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:283, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:285. Keep Marisol's analytic cast of mind, but vary how motive and correction reach the page: let some beats resolve through action or sensory image, and let at least two of the cited negation ladders land as blunt positive statements instead of qualifying themselves into place.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:351 each read as complete narrative sentences with explicit subjects and verbs where needed; FAIL if any of those moments still depend on one-word narration or omitted-verbless shorthand.
evidence: Repeated clipped narration appears at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:351.
problem: The chapter repeatedly drops into clipped narrative shorthand such as "A swallow," verbless countdown fragments, and one-word reaction beats. That cadence conflicts with the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping and starts to read as mannerism rather than earned compression.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:235, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:351, recast the fragment beats as fully grammatical narrative sentences while preserving speed and pressure. Keep the same phone-call, countdown, threat, guard, and timing functions, but give each beat a complete clause instead of relying on clipped shorthand.

acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one of the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285 still begins with a demonstrative reaction sentence and each revised beat is distinguishable on the page through a different physical, spatial, or behavioral response; FAIL if multiple cited beats still rely on the same "That ..." interpretive construction.
evidence: A repeated demonstrative-response formula appears at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285.
problem: Several leverage shifts are translated through the same interpretive sentence shape, opening with "That ..." to explain how a line lands. Used this often, the formula becomes a prose tic and slightly over-explains moments that the chapter could otherwise land through sensation, action, or silence.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:173, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:285, vary or cut the demonstrative reaction openings. Replace at least three of those beats with distinct renderings rooted in Marisol's body, the room, or the other character's behavior so each turn of pressure lands in a different sentence shape.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:45-57 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:97-101 no longer deliver back-to-back explanatory paragraphs about what the hold 'means' after the harm is already shown, and if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:331-333 ends on debt or consequence without restating the same moral verdict a third time. Fail if the revision still explains Marisol's culpability immediately after the scene has already made it legible through observed damage.
problem: The chapter already renders the hold's worker and detainee cost vividly on the page, but it then repeatedly steps in to explain Marisol's guilt and the system's meaning. That repetition starts to act like a moral safety valve: the scene keeps telling the reader that the sabotage is ethically compromised instead of trusting the concrete harm and Gabriel's later rebuke to carry the indictment.
rewrite_direction: Compress or cut one explanatory beat in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:45-57 and another in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:97-101 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:331-333. Keep the strongest material consequence, then replace the weaker summary with fresh scene pressure at the same local span: a worker's operational problem, a detainee's immediate bodily cost, or a tactical choice Marisol makes to preserve the hold. Let Gabriel's exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:253-311 do more of the explicit ethical naming.

acceptance_test: Pass if at least three of the cited beats are rewritten without the current 'as if' explanatory hedge and each revised sentence reveals state through a distinct concrete action or sensory fact. Fail if Ivo's hesitation, the girl's endurance, and Marisol's cover behavior are still rendered through substantially the same look-plus-comparison construction.
problem: Several key beats rely on the same look-based, 'as if' inferential construction to tell the reader what hesitation, concealment, or endurance resembles. By the back half, that repeated sentence habit starts to smooth distinct emotional situations into one tonal texture, which is below the chapter's otherwise high level of specificity.
rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated inferential beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:143, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:153, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:245, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:249, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:309 so at least several of them land through direct physical fact instead of another 'as if' frame. acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:47 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:69 contains at least two Marisol-centered beats of thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, and no run longer than five consecutive paragraphs plays without focalizer interiority. Fail if the tape still reads as an uninterrupted Q-and-A block until line 69.
problem: The first recorder block runs as an extended transcript-like exchange before the narration returns to Marisol at line 69. Because the chapter is otherwise close third, this 47-67 stretch drops her thought, bodily listening, memory, and self-assessment long enough for the scene to flatten away from lived focalization.
rewrite_direction: Re-anchor the recorder sequence inside Marisol's body and working mind by inserting close-third beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:47, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:57, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:65 before the return at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:69. acceptance_test: Pass if those revised lines contain no more than two negation-correction constructions of the form 'not X, just Y,' 'It was not X. It was just Y,' or equivalent near-variants, while the physical fact in each beat remains immediately legible. Fail if three or more such self-correcting pivots remain in the cited spans.
problem: A negation-then-correction sentence shape becomes a visible tic: 'not X, just Y' or near-variants recur across the chapter's pivots. After the third instance, the construction stops feeling chosen and starts announcing the prose's own self-correction pattern.
rewrite_direction: Vary the landings at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:153, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:183, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:207. acceptance_test: PASS if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:247, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:271, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:356, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:357 lands the same humiliation/tightening/isolation beats in complete clauses; FAIL if any of those moments still depend on fragment-only lines with omitted auxiliaries or bare noun-phrase sentence endings.
problem: The chapter repeatedly uses clipped narrative fragments to force emphasis: "Manifest editing gone," "Not louder. Tighter.", "Undocumented favors. Personal improvisations.", and the final "Not Teresa's speech. Not the orange strip. The space." Because this pattern recurs across all three scenes and at the closing beat, it reads as a chapter-wide compression tic instead of a deliberate one-off effect, and it violates the constitution's explicit ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
rewrite_direction: Recast the emphatic fragments at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:247, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:271, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:356, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:357 into short but grammatically complete clauses. Preserve the pressure and cadence, but stop omitting auxiliaries or relying on bare noun phrases for impact.

acceptance_test: PASS if rereading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:211, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:347 shows that at least four of those beats now land without `as if`, and the remaining comparison(s) feel singular rather than habitual; FAIL if those passages still rely on the same repeated comparative scaffold to move from observation into meaning.
problem: The chapter leans on `as if` comparison scaffolding often enough that it starts to pre-shape the prose. The junior clerk, Marisol's bodily metaphor, Paloma's diagnosis, Rufino's shoe check, Ivo's posture, and the attendants' avoidance all arrive through the same comparative hinge, which flattens sentence variety in an otherwise strong chapter.
rewrite_direction: Rework the weaker `as if` constructions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:211, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:347, and keep only the comparison(s) that truly earn metaphorical lift. acceptance_test: FAIL if the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:63-64, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:139-145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:189-190, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:205-206, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:269-271, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:337-343, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:419 contain no more than one repeated 'not X / it was Y' reversal across the set. PASS if at least three of those targeted beats now state the concrete reality directly without a negation setup while preserving the same narrative meaning.
problem: The prose relies too often on the same negation-and-correction emphasis scaffold ('not X ... Y' / 'It was not X. It was Y.'). Repeated this many times in one chapter, the move stops sharpening pressure and starts to feel like an authorial tic that pre-explains beats the scene has already earned.
rewrite_direction: Revise the highlighted pivots at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:63, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:139, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:205, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:269, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:337, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:419 so most of those beats land in direct sensory or judgment language instead of being defined by what they are not. acceptance_test: Pass if the revised speech at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:293, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:297, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 still links the bathroom clutter to the line's accumulated harm and still asks Ivo for a hearing, but does so without a finished aphorism and without the exact phrase "breaks first under strain". Fail if Marisol still sounds like she is delivering the chapter's thesis instead of speaking from immediate pressure.
problem: Marisol's key explanatory lines tip from pressured worker speech into polished thematic delivery. The room analogy resolves too neatly, and the exact "breaks first under strain" phrasing reads like style-bible calibration leaking into dialogue rather than Marisol's own rough, task-first register.
rewrite_direction: Rework Marisol's spoken turns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:293, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:297, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 so the thought stays grounded in task logic, residue, and self-correction. Keep the dentures/room comparison and the request for a hearing, but replace the slogan-like line and the exact calibration phrase with scene-specific rail or household language Marisol would plausibly reach for under pressure.

acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised pivots anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:255, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:337, no more than two beats use look/looked/looked away as the main emotional action and the other cited turns are carried by visibly different bodily or sensory actions. Fail if the scene still relies on the same gaze beat more than twice.
problem: The chapter marks too many emotional pivots with the same gaze-direction construction: characters look at each other, look at the paper, look away toward the train, or look back to the dorm windows. After multiple repetitions, the staging starts to feel mechanically managed instead of freshly embodied.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:337, replace at least three of the repeated look/looked-away pivots with distinct physical business tied to the setting and character state, such as the spoon flex, cigarette pass, taped hand, gutter runoff, coffee heat, or Marisol's knee. acceptance_test: In the revised handoff beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:315 and resolving by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:355, the packet reaches the courier only after an explicit on-page barrier changes the risk in the room. If Lidia moves directly from recognition to dispatch with no new complication, this fails.
problem: The archive handoff states urgency and risk, but once Lidia recognizes the packet the climax turns into mostly frictionless procedure. That undercuts scene pressure at the exact point the chapter needs the leak to feel contested and earned.
rewrite_direction: Rework the archive sequence around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:315, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:325, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:355 so Marisol has to beat one concrete obstacle after Lidia understands the evidence: a credibility check, routing problem, courier delay, or archive-protocol demand that costs time, exposure, or leverage before the pouch leaves.

acceptance_test: In the revised archive scene anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:321 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:375, at least two of Lidia's major lines are grounded in archival process or transit logistics rather than stand-alone maxims, and she does not deliver two consecutive quotable aphorisms. If her voice still reads as generic house wit, this fails.
problem: Lidia's dialogue arrives in a polished aphoristic register that blurs idiolect separation in the late scene. Lines like 'make your bad theft less stupid' and 'leave separately from your fear' sound authored-to-quote rather than overheard from a specific archivist, so the final scene briefly shares the same sharp-house voice already used by Gabriel and Paloma.
rewrite_direction: Retune Lidia's key lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:321, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:343, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:375 toward archival procedure, courier practice, and institutional risk. Keep at most one epigrammatic line; let the rest carry a more task-shaped syntax that distinguishes her from Gabriel's cutting brevity and Paloma's medical plainness.

acceptance_test: In the revised reveal passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:191, Marisol names the scheme once and then the scene turns decisively to copying or escape. In the revised closing anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:377 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:383, the final paragraphs add a new tactical or sensory consequence rather than a second abstract definition of the same secret. If both passages still restate the profit model in new synonyms, this fails.
problem: After the invoice reveal, the chapter re-explains the same discovery in several adjacent conceptual summaries: secondary market, side door, dirt outside company custody, dirtiest secret crossing a threshold. The repetitions are individually strong, but stacked together they blunt the shock and slow the chapter's otherwise hard-moving investigative rhythm.
rewrite_direction: Compress the reflective restatement around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:191, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:193, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:197 so one sharp realization carries the forced-labor mechanism, then spend the recovered space on action or a fresh bodily consequence. Do the same for the close at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:377 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:383 so the ending advances consequence instead of rephrasing the same insight.

acceptance_test: Pass if, by reading the revised chapter, the reader gets each of these only once in full: the stoppage map, the no-private-rescue principle, and the public-testimony plan. In the Car 8 scene starting at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:253, at least two new operational constraints must appear before any recap of earlier tactics, and the beat corresponding to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:387 must add a new consequence rather than restating the one-person-miracle warning.
problem: The chapter's preparation engine is sound, but too much of the page count is spent re-stating it. Scene one builds the stoppage map by classification, then scene two repeats most of that map before it reaches Gabriel's genuinely new contributions about door order, witness choreography, and the recorder. The ethical line against a private rescue is also stated, then restated, instead of turning immediately into fresh obstacle or leverage. The result is intermittent strategy-memo drag in a chapter that should feel like tightening conspiracy.
rewrite_direction: Compress or refunction the list-making that begins at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:7 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:19 so the chapter reaches the irrevocable commitments sooner. In the car scene, cut the recap launch at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:253 and move faster to Gabriel's new constraints at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:285 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:351. Once Marisol states terms at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:173, later beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:215 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:387 should introduce new risk, timing, or sacrifice rather than another formulation of the same no-private-ladder principle.

acceptance_test: Pass if each revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:317, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:399 ends on a concrete image, action, or consequence rather than an abstract paraphrase of theme, and if none of those paragraphs merely restates what the immediately preceding dialogue already made legible.
problem: Several paragraphs stop after strong concrete material and explain the institutional or moral meaning the reader has already understood. The commentary is intelligent, but in aggregate it softens the chapter's hardness by paraphrasing its own subtext instead of trusting the scene evidence.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:317, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:399, either cut the thematic gloss outright or replace it with a fresh sensory or social consequence grounded in Marisol's body, the room, or the objects already present. Let the invoice, wrapper, gate, passcard, and platform logistics carry the indictment instead of summarizing what the company or plan means.

acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:123, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:185, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:395, no more than two still use the current not-X/but-or-only-Y construction and the replacements remain clear without relying on the same binary pivot.
problem: The prose leans repeatedly on the same binary-negation hinge: not X, but Y; not X, only Y. Used once, the pattern sharpens emphasis. Repeated across the chapter, it becomes audible as a house tic and flattens sentence music.
rewrite_direction: Recast at least three of the repeated hinges at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:123, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:185, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:395 into different sentence shapes driven by image, action, or cadence rather than denial-then-correction. Keep no more than one or two of the current hinges if you want the pattern to survive as a deliberate choice.

acceptance_test: Pass if, by reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:13 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:143-149, a reader can point to one explicit sentence before the assay and one explicit sentence during Teresa's intervention showing that the transfer workaround is either a novel abuse or a consciously accepted risk. Fail if Teresa's move still feels like an obvious clause Marisol should already have planned for.
problem: The chapter's central backfire is not fully earned causally. Marisol and Paloma build the pathogen gambit around the assumption that an amber hold will force outside review before reconciliation, but Teresa defeats them with an emergency-variance clause Marisol explicitly already knew. Because the text does not establish why Marisol discounted that clause or why Teresa's use of it is a novel abuse, the catastrophe reads partly as protagonist oversight the plot needs rather than pressure the chapter has fully modeled.
rewrite_direction: Revise the setup at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:13 and the countermeasure reveal at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:143, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:147, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:149 so the reader understands why the hold looked binding before Teresa moved. Options: establish a concrete legal or operational condition that should have blocked split-consist transfer until Teresa bad-faiths it in real time, or make Marisol name the variance risk before the assay and proceed anyway because another clock makes the gamble unavoidable. Do not leave the reversal resting on a policy tool she knew and simply failed to model.

acceptance_test: Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:187, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:335, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:559-561, no more than one beat uses a bookkeeping or neatness metaphor and the other cited beats are distinguishable through concrete sensory detail. Fail if multiple cited passages still summarize the catastrophe as the day becoming orderly, neat, or columned.
problem: The prose repeats the same bookkeeping/neatness abstraction four times: the day arranging itself in clean columns, the chapter of the day opening, the death slotting into the day with obscene neatness, and the property-marking keeping the day from going neat. That recurrence turns a useful motif into a tic and starts explaining catastrophe through managerial summary instead of letting concrete bodily and rail-specific consequence carry the pressure.
rewrite_direction: Keep at most one version of this abstraction and revise the others at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:187, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:335, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:559 so each beat lands through different concrete material: heat, metal, paperwork residue, bodily aftermath, or sound. The replacement language should stay inside Marisol's close-third sensibility without returning to 'day,' 'clean columns,' or 'neat' as the default frame.

acceptance_test: Reading the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:313, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:371, no more than one beat may still use the 'Not X. [Correction Y]' construction. The remaining beats must deliver the same emotional information through visibly different sentence shapes rather than paired negation.
problem: The chapter reuses the same corrective-negation mini-structure ('Not X. Y.' / 'Not because X. Because Y.') often enough that it stops reading as emphasis and starts reading as chapter-level fallback. That repeated sentence shape flattens otherwise strong emotional beats by making several turns land in the same rhythm.
rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated negation beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:313, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_13.md:371. Keep at most the single strongest instance; express the other turns through distinct syntax grounded in fresh image, bodily sensation, or action so each beat lands on its own terms.

acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:349, the revised sentence contains no chapter-number or drafting-stage reference. A cold reader can still understand that Marisol recognizes the coding from prior experience entirely through diegetic language.
problem: The narration explicitly says "back in chapter ten," which is process leakage rather than story-world thought. It breaks diegesis at the exact point the property-room evidence should feel most embodied and violates the constitution's ban on process leakage in narrative text.
rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:349 so Marisol recognizes the secondary-market codes through in-world memory or operational recall instead of chapter numbering. Keep the same factual linkage to the municipal-deferral scheme, but phrase it as something she learned earlier on the line, in copied ledgers, or during the July records pull.

acceptance_test: In the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:221, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:273, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:353, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:355, each narrative sentence has an explicit subject and finite verb. None of those beats remains as a bare noun/adjective fragment such as "Clean plastic" or "Valve. Exception. Discretion."
problem: The chapter repeatedly lands emphasis through clipped narration fragments and noun-phrase bursts: "Underfed, bruised, twenty-seven," "Fourteen thousand two hundred dollars," "Valve. Exception. Discretion.," "Clean plastic. Fresh print labels. Priority treatment.," and the bag inventory at Gabriel's shelf. Once or twice could read as pressure; repeated this often, it becomes a visible prose tic and collides with the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
rewrite_direction: Rework the narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:29, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:221, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:273, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:353, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:355 into grammatically complete sentences with explicit subjects and verbs. Preserve the pressure by varying sentence length and cadence, not by dropping sentence grammar.

acceptance_test: Across the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:219, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:251, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:405, Teresa, Ivo, and Marisol are each characterized at least once without reusing cuticles/glasses, bird-guide page/burn scar, or wrist-scar shorthand. The replacement details must expose current pressure through action or perception visible on the page.
problem: The chapter repeatedly reaches for the cast's preloaded calibration tells instead of fresher scene-specific observation: Gabriel's eyebrow scar and bruised ribs, Marisol's wrist scar, Teresa's bitten cuticles and misplaced glasses, Ivo's bird-guide page and burn scar. Because these are the exact dossier markers already attached to the characters, replaying them this densely starts to feel checklisted and slightly flattens otherwise strong voice work.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:79 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:219 for Teresa, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:49 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:405 for Ivo, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:69 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:251 for Marisol, replace at least one repeated calibration tell per character with a new behavior, sensory reaction, or work habit generated by the current scene. Keep continuity, but stop reintroducing pressure through the same stock identifiers.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:78, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:128, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:330 contains at least two concrete reminders that Gabriel is physically trapped and endangered, and at least one of those reminders changes Marisol's attention or decision in the scene; FAIL if Gabriel remains opening-setup only and then disappears into prop status.
problem: The chapter establishes Gabriel hidden in the display cabinet as a live bodily risk, then lets the investor-demo comedy run for most of the chapter without returning that pressure. That weakens the requirement to keep the underlying cruelty visible and makes Gabriel read more like contraband scenery than a trapped person whose body is paying for the sabotage.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:304 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:305 uses complete clauses with explicit subjects and verbs for each image beat while preserving the fast reveal through the door; FAIL if the passage still relies on noun-phrase fragments.
problem: The glimpse into the next car switches into clipped noun-phrase narration: 'Rows of actual bunks. A mother lifting a paper cup... Naila near the aisle...' acceptance_test: PASS if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:102, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:308, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:322, no more than two Teresa reaction beats use the mask-slip or mask-return construction and at least one later beat shows pressure through a different register such as misplacing an object, over-correcting language, or issuing a visibly compromised order; FAIL if each new Teresa setback still arrives through the same face-reset formula.
problem: Teresa's reversals are described four times through the same mask-slip-then-reset facial beat: the smile clips into place, the polish leaves and returns, the color leaves and comes back as authority, then the smile returns again. The repetition turns a useful motif into a tic and flattens a character whose stress tells should vary more than this chapter allows.
rewrite_direction: Vary the Teresa-pressure beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:102, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:308, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:322 so at least one later stress response lands through object handling, syntax, or command behavior instead of another facial-mask reset.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised ending anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:343 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:353 contains only one explicit explanatory confirmation of the stoppage before landing on a concrete on-platform beat; FAIL if the passage still repeats the same authority-shift/theme in multiple summary sentences after the decisive hold is already clear.
problem: After the decisive civic lock lands, the chapter re-states the rupture several times in explanatory summary. The inspector's 'city too' turn, Marisol's internal confirmation, the accomplice-cost checklist, and the final thesis sentence all point at the same state change, so the climax loses some of its ferocity by explaining what the scene has already made legible.
rewrite_direction: Compress and sharpen the exit around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:343, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:347, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:353. Keep one decisive confirmation that the stoppage will hold, then end on an immediate platform consequence, image, or spoken line instead of another abstract statement of what the system meant or what authority has become.

acceptance_test: PASS if, in the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:259 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:277, the reader understands within two sentences of Paloma's entrance that she is carrying original trauma-room evidence with city-backed storage proof, and Teresa/inspector reaction is not interrupted by a long route-reconstruction aside; FAIL if the scene still stops to narrate how Paloma got through the crowd before the reveal lands.
problem: Paloma's cooler entrance is materially important, but the narration pauses the live crisis to backfill how she stashed it, how she crossed the commuter gate, and why the chain of custody works. In a scene whose power comes from public momentum, that amount of retroactive logistics makes the reveal feel author-managed instead of explosively present.
rewrite_direction: Rework the reveal at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:259, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:269, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:277 so the beat plays in real time: Paloma arrives, Teresa and the inspectors react, the cooler opens, and the municipal chit/protected contents prove legitimacy. Trim the commuter-gate reconstruction to one short clause or move that explanation elsewhere if it must remain.

acceptance_test: PASS if, across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:225, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:265, no more than two still use the explicit negative-definition setup before naming the real effect; FAIL if three or more keep the same 'not X, but Y' construction.
problem: A negative-definition sentence shape recurs enough to read as formula: the prose repeatedly tells the reader what a moment is not before stating what it is. Used this often, the correction pattern becomes predictable and slightly self-canceling in a chapter that otherwise wants harder forward drive.
rewrite_direction: Vary at least two of the cited beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:225, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:265. Lead with the concrete effect, or compress the contrast into a single sentence, rather than opening each beat with an explicit 'it/he was not' correction.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:223 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:229 contains Gabriel's exact raw utterance, or equivalently concrete free-indirect phrasing, plus a sensory or social trace of the outburst; fail if the moment can still be paraphrased as narrated swearing followed by a neutral line.
problem: The chapter sanitizes Gabriel's sharpest reaction beat. When Marisol reports that Paloma is being held and her visa is under review, the draft retreats into narrated swearing ("He swore under his breath") and a cleaned-up reply instead of rendering the anger directly on the page, which softens both the political retaliation and Gabriel's pressure response.
rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:227, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:229 so Gabriel's reaction is rendered in exact on-page language and bodily residue rather than summarized as swearing. Keep the beat specific to Paloma's detention and rough enough to satisfy the constitution's anti-sanitization rule.

acceptance_test: Pass if, by reading the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:91, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:257, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:311, a reader can distinguish Ivo from Gabriel by rhythm and roughness alone, and each man has at least one high-pressure line with character-appropriate coarse language; fail if both still sound equally controlled and profanity-free.
problem: Ivo and Gabriel are both written cleaner than their style-bible pressure registers. Ivo's crushed-foot and wage-loss lines stay almost entirely tidy and non-profane despite a high-profanity profile, while Gabriel's immigration and witness-risk language also stays polished. The result is partial idiolect flattening: two distinct men under extreme stress land in a similarly controlled literary register.
rewrite_direction: Rework Ivo's pressure beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:49 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:91, plus Gabriel's beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:229, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:257, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:311. Let Ivo roughen through mechanic deadpan and character-specific profanity, and let Gabriel sharpen into his own harsher documentary anger rather than the same polished clarity.

acceptance_test: Pass if, after Gabriel raises leaving tonight at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:247, the revised exchange through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:303 uses no more than one bare acknowledgment reply and every later turn either adds a new concrete fact, names a new cost, or changes what the other person concedes; fail if the scene still cycles through repeated "I know" variants.
problem: The bakery scene starts to loop on mutual-acknowledgment replies. Repeated variants of "I know" and "I didn't say you didn't" begin answering the wound without changing leverage, which blunts the witness-choice scene just as it should keep deepening.
rewrite_direction: Tighten the exchange centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:241, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:243, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:265, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:269, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:301, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:303. Keep one acknowledgment loop if needed, then replace the later repetitions with new admissions, refusals, silences, or concrete memories that materially alter what the other person understands.

acceptance_test: Pass if no more than one of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:313 still uses an explicit negation/redefinition formula to explain the emotional turn; fail if all three beats keep the same explanatory construction.
problem: The chapter repeats the same interpretive sentence shape at major emotional hinges: negating one reading and then restating the "real" one. By the third iteration, the prose starts sounding pre-authored rather than discovered, and it explains feelings the surrounding scene already carries.
rewrite_direction: Recast at least two of the hinges at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:189, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:295, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:313 so they land through image, gesture, or a different sentence shape.
